title: Smoky Roasted Welsh Lamb Roast
description: Charred and delicious, this traditional Welsh lamb roast is perfect for a special occasion. Serve with roasted root vegetables for a hearty meal.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine lamb shoulder, chopped onion, chopped thyme, chopped rosemary, garlic powder, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper. Mix well to combine.
Place the lamb shoulder in a large roasting pan and put in the oven.

Roast the lamb for 20 minutes, then reduce the oven temperature to 180°C (350°F) and continue roasting for a further 2 hours and 20 minutes, or until the lamb is tender and the internal temperature reaches 63°C (145°F).
While the lamb is roasting, prepare the glaze by combining 2 tablespoons of honey, 1 tablespoon of Dijon mustard, and 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a small bowl.
After the lamb has roasted for 2 hours, brush the glaze all over the lamb and continue roasting for a further 20 minutes.
Remove the lamb from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve the lamb with the juices from the roasting pan spooned over the top.
Optional: serve with roasted root vegetables such as carrots, parsnips, and Brussels sprouts.
